businessSummary.oneLiner: Leading AI data analytics platform for government and enterprise, built on three pillars: Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI-powered data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%). U.S. commercial revenue surpassed $500M. The Pentagon adopted Maven AI as an official program of record, rolling it out across the entire U.S. military. FY2026 guidance calls for $7.19B to $7.41B in revenue (+60-65%), signaling accelerating growth.
howTheyMakeMoney: Government contracts (Gotham) - Defense and intelligence AI analytics
Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - Large-scale data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - Generative AI integration platform
AIP Boot Camps - Short-term adoption programs driving customer conversion

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: About 10x the S&P 500 average P/E (23-25x) and 6x the software sector average (35-40x). An extreme premium, but Forward P/E drops to 113x based on FY2026 EPS of $1.34

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 estimated EPS of $1.34. Still expensive, but with 65% revenue growth, PEG ratio of 2.48 falls within high-growth territory

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue of $4.48B. AI demand explosion drove 56% year-over-year growth. FY2026 guidance targets 65% growth

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier profitability among software companies. Combined with 82% gross margin, demonstrates strong earnings power

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Free cash flow of $2.10B. Exceptional cash generation with minimal debt ($229M)

keyMetrics.name: Cash Position
keyMetrics.interpretation: Net cash of $6.95B ($2.90 per share). Near debt-free balance sheet

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Backlog
keyMetrics.interpretation: Provides multi-year revenue visibility. Roughly 2.5x current annual revenue

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI Formally Adopted Across U.S. Military
growthDrivers.description: Deputy Secretary of Defense designated Maven Smart System as an official program of record via memo on March 9. Long-term deployment across all military branches secures stable defense revenue

growthDrivers.title: Combat-Proven AI in Iran Operations
growthDrivers.description: NYT reported Palantir operates a system analyzing Pentagon intelligence using Anthropic AI technology. Battlefield validation serves as powerful evidence for additional contracts

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Guidance: $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%)
growthDrivers.description: Management projects accelerating growth. U.S. commercial segment emerging as the key growth engine, reducing government dependency

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M + Army $10B Contracts
growthDrivers.description: Major military contracts secured in succession. $11.2B revenue backlog provides long-term revenue visibility

growthDrivers.title: Analyst Price Target Upgrades
growthDrivers.description: UBS raised to $200 (Strong Buy upgrade), Wedbush maintains $230, Rosenblatt raised from $150 to $200. Wall Street broadly turning more bullish

growthDrivers.title: AI Boot Camp Accelerating Commercial Adoption
growthDrivers.description: Short-term trial programs proving effective at rapid customer acquisition. U.S. commercial revenue surpassed $500M

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, top-tier security clearances, Maven military adoption, and NVIDIA sovereign AI partnership. Combat deployment in Iran operations establishes an overwhelming verification advantage over competitors

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government and military systems. Once deployed, replacement costs are prohibitively high
Highest-level security clearances and 20+ years of government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to achieve equivalent access
Rapid customer acquisition through AIP Boot Camps and expanding Ontology data model ecosystem
Used for real-time target identification in Iran operations. Battlefield data is itself a competitive advantage

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Emerging defense AI/drone company. Hardware-focused, making it more complementary than competitive with Palantir's software platform

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competitors. Significantly behind Palantir in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants expanding into government AI. Scale advantage exists but matching Palantir's deep Ontology platform integration remains difficult

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Trails Palantir significantly in both revenue scale and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Adopts Maven AI as Core U.S. Military System
recentNews.significance: Deputy Secretary of Defense designated Maven Smart System as program of record. Long-term contract across all military branches stabilizes defense revenue

recentNews.headline: Silicon Valley Defense Bets Pay Off, Palantir AI Deployed in Iran Operations
recentNews.significance: NYT report. Anthropic AI + Palantir system analyzing Pentagon intelligence in active combat. Real-world AI validation

recentNews.headline: UBS Raises Target to $200 from $180 (Strong Buy)
recentNews.significance: Analyst Karl Keirstead raised target 11% citing accelerating revenue and Maven adoption

recentNews.headline: $11.2B Revenue Backlog, $10B Army Contract, 61% Growth Outlook
recentNews.significance: FY2026 revenue guidance projects 61% growth. Large military contracts and backlog secure long-term visibility

recentNews.headline: Simply Wall St Analyzes PLTR Valuation Based on AI Contracts and Partnerships
recentNews.significance: Expanding partnerships with NVIDIA, GE Aerospace, and Moderna. Confirms commercial segment acceleration

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS Karl Keirstead raised target to $200 maintaining Strong Buy. Wedbush Dan Ives holds $230 target citing accelerating AI defense demand. Rosenblatt also raised significantly from $150 to $200

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: TTM P/E of 239x is roughly 10x the S&P 500 average. Significant downside risk if growth decelerates. Michael Burry previously flagged a head-and-shoulders pattern warning

risks.title: War-Dependent Revenue Risk
risks.description: Defense revenue surged with Iran combat deployment, but growth momentum could weaken if the conflict ends. War-driven growth raises sustainability concerns

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: Share count increased 4.67% YoY. Stock-based compensation structure steadily dilutes existing shareholder value

risks.title: Government Budget Volatility
risks.description: U.S. government shutdowns or defense budget cuts would directly impact revenue. Government revenue share remains significant

risks.title: Ethical and Reputational Risk
risks.description: Growing criticism of AI weapons targeting systems. Swiss magazine lawsuit and press freedom controversies add pressure

risks.title: Intensifying Competition
risks.description: Microsoft, AWS and other cloud giants entering government AI market. Limited near-term threat but requires long-term monitoring

buyReasons.title: Undisputed Leader in Defense AI
buyReasons.rationale: Maven formally adopted as a U.S. military system. Over 20 years of government trust and security clearances are assets money cannot buy

buyReasons.title: Growth Is Accelerating
buyReasons.rationale: From +56% in FY2025 to +65% guidance for FY2026. Revenue growth is speeding up, not slowing down

buyReasons.title: Commercial Segment Exploding
buyReasons.rationale: U.S. commercial revenue surpassed $500M. AIP Boot Camps rapidly expanding the enterprise customer base, reducing government dependency in a healthy structural shift

buyReasons.title: Fortress Balance Sheet
buyReasons.rationale: Cash $7.18B, debt $229M, FCF margin 47%. Strong cash generation, minimal debt, overflowing reserves

buyReasons.title: Wall Street Targets Rising
buyReasons.rationale: Consensus target $196 (+30% upside). Major brokerages including UBS and Rosenblatt raising targets in succession

overallOpinion: Maven AI has been formally adopted as a core U.S. military system. Combined with combat deployment in Iran, Palantir has cemented its dominant position in the defense AI market
Commercial revenue topped $500M, backlog stands at $11.2B, and FY2026 guidance calls for +65% growth. Partnerships with NVIDIA, GE Aerospace and others continue expanding
That said, a P/E of 239x carries significant risk. Investors can buy into the growth story, but a single earnings miss could trigger a sharp selloff
Technically trading below both the 50-day and 200-day moving averages with RSI at 51, neutral. A near-term pullback zone, but fundamental momentum remains strong

investmentVerdict.summary: Maven's formal military adoption and combat deployment in Iran have confirmed Palantir's unrivaled position in defense AI. FY2026 +65% growth guidance and commercial acceleration underpin strong fundamentals. However, a 239x P/E valuation warrants caution, making a dollar-cost averaging strategy on pullbacks the prudent approach.

x.hook: Maven adopted by US military, P/E 239x dilemma
x.text:
$PLTR (3/23) Maven AI officially adopted as core US military system. Combat-tested in Iran. Closed at $150.68(-3.21%).

1/4
thread:
Commercial revenue past $500M, backlog $11.2B, FY2026 guidance +65%. Partnerships with NVIDIA, GE Aerospace expanding.

2/4

The problem: P/E 239x. That's 6x the software sector average. Growth is fast, but one miss and it drops hard.

3/4

Below 50/200-day MAs, RSI 51 neutral. Target $196(+30.4%). Strong fundamentals, rich valuation. Buy on dips.

4/4

witty.hook: Winning wars, losing in the stock market
witty.text:
$PLTR (3/23) AI combat-tested in Iran, adopted by the US military. Stock? $150.68, down 3.21%. Wins the war, loses the trading day.

1/4

empathy.hook: Good news, falling stock. PLTR holder's dilemma
empathy.text:
$PLTR (3/23) $150.68(-3.21%)

Best-ever news cycle: Maven adoption, Iran deployment. Yet the stock fell. If you're holding, your feelings are complicated.

Commercial $500M, backlog $11.2B, +65% growth. Fundamentals are peak. But P/E 239x means one stumble = big drop. That anxiety is real.

Target $196(+30.4%). Believe in the long game? Pullbacks are your friend. Scale in, keep sizing modest.
